title: allow portions of buildah to build for windows
body:
the podman remote client for windows needs to be able to compile certain
portions of buildah for windows.  this seems like the safest way to not
break api for buildah.

as podman matures, we might be able to avoid this by not compiling some
of the core pieces of libpod.  that is a longer term process however.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Much simpler patch would be to just add

// +build linux to existing files and them implement // +build -linux to implement the non supported ones.
So I'm curious why podman remote needs to get to Buildah run functionality?  I was expecting to see something closer to @rhatdan 's earlier PR.

General comments:  try moving `Run()` and all of its support functions (the ones like `addCommonOptsToSpec()` that would compile for other platforms, but still expect to manipulate paths in /proc, which is Linux-specific, so we're not saving ourselves any trouble by compiling them on other platforms) into `run_linux.go`.  When this is done, `run.go` should only contain type definitions and functions for manipulating those types that libpod needs in order to compile.

Instead of naming files with _supported as a suffix and using a build constraint to only build the file on Linux, use _linux in the filename.
